examples: Whole rest as compositional intention, not absence
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Rest / structured silence in notation. The space between sounds carries information.

examples: Landing call at heronry triggers mate response
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Foundational structure of jazz improvisation, gospel, West African drumming traditions.

examples: Bass register as harmonic root in orchestration
Cello/bassoon range — the voice that defines the key
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Bass voice provides harmonic foundation. Degree 0 IS the root note of the chord.

examples: 0-1 acoustic events per hour vs. Pacific Wren 120+ notes/min
Slower than Largo (40-66 BPM) — geological tempo
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The slowest tempo on the circle. Every faster rhythm at higher degrees is a subdivision of this silence.

examples: Estuarine fishing strategy — patience as hunting technique
Strike velocity: 60+ body lengths per second
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Energy efficiency as compositional economy. Maximum effect from minimum events.
Solo instrument (silence) vs. full ensemble (texture). Dynamic range of behavioral context.

examples: Bill clapping — percussion without vocal apparatus
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Percussion — sound produced by physical impact rather than vocal/reed vibration.
